gate: canonical fixed-point regeneration (SP2 deferred integration step)

attempt_1.failure: ReferenceResolutionError: BB_WORKSPACE_ROOT is required for workspace evidence references; checkout=.../breadboard_repo_integration_main_20260326
attempt_1.classification: intentional F5/AM19 contract: workspace evidence references must be explicitly rooted, not ambient-resolved; fail-closed without the variable is the designed behavior (reviewed in ReviewF5 scope: run_lane/path_refs/readiness resolver consumers)

attempt_2.failure: shutil.Error in _prepare_candidate_root (regenerate_evidence.py:972): full-checkout copytree hit dangling symlink misc/codex_cli_runs/goldens/codex_cli_0.139.0_default_model/codex_cli_default_model_smoke/current and stale .venv_linux_import_20260618 entries
attempt_2.classification: SP2 integration defect: candidate-root preparation not robust to real-checkout hazards (dangling symlinks, stale venvs); packet tests used synthetic trees. Rollback semantics held (no partial promotion observed - failure occurred before promotion).

hermeticity_qualification: A pass with BB_WORKSPACE_ROOT set certifies the accepted-tree fixed point GIVEN the declared workspace root; references into that root are legitimate only where digest-pinned (F5 tracked ledger 908cd376 pattern) or tombstoned. Reviewer of the integration-generated commit must verify which workspace references the regen consumed and that each is pinned or justified.
attempt_3_plan: PacketSP2 fix on p20/sp2b from b84c759e: symlinks-as-symlinks + explicit non-evidence ignore set + regression test; then rerun

attempt_3.integration_sha: d1a0b20c0aac (post SP2b merge; SP2b reviewed APPROVED @ ffceba6b)
attempt_3.determinism_result: candidate-root defect RESOLVED: two full passes byte-identical (snapshot sha256:1ae94011b596bb27..., watch_set_size 1163, first/second exit both 1). The regen DAG is deterministic; failure is a persistent failing stage, not fixed-point divergence.
attempt_3.failure_family: PIN_STALE in lane breadboard_self_runtime_records_v1: accepted support claims/evidence manifests pin historic bytes - atomic_feature_ledger pinned a001fe69 (the corrupt ambient-seed digest documented in f5_seed_digest_resolution.json), ledger_row_refs row hash mismatch for feat_e7bfda921c4e30ed (the exact sidecar-stale row F5 classified), plus self-capture pins (raw_capture_manifest, target_probe_output, runtime_records/*, replay, comparator) stale against fresh capture bytes. Pipeline itself rewrites docs_tmp/phase_15 seeds (ledger seed -> f218f14b, source index -> f96716b7) before the lane validates old pins.
attempt_3.classification: pre-existing ambient-reseed consumer residual, product_defect family of F5 seed-digest mechanism: the north-star self-runtime lane still consumes/validates the ambient phase-15 seed and prior self-capture bytes instead of the F5 tracked canonical ledger (908cd376) route. Not introduced by SP2b (previous attempts never reached this stage). Ownership decision folded into the Wave-B/SP6 scope decision at post-Wave-A SHA per wave_b_hermeticity_diagnostic.required_followup.

attempt_4.failing_stage: north_star_proof_packets, 3 PIN_STALE errors on runtime_records/records/bb.kernel_event.v2.jsonl (expected 4a4fe71c = accepted, got 33e2aa4a)
attempt_4.invalidated_by: attempt_5 root-cause finding: the DAG subprocesses executed stale wsD5 code, not the merged SP6 fix

attempt_5_root_cause.finding: ENVIRONMENT DEFECT: shared integration .venv contained editable install breadboard_harness_cli-0.0.0 (installed 2026-07-10 20:35, M1 wsD5 static-verification era) whose meta-path finder mapped six top-level packages (scripts, breadboard, agentic_coder_prototype, breadboard_sdk, conformance, adaptive_iter) to the STALE worktree /Users/kylemccleary/projects/breadboard/docs_tmp/phase_20/worktrees/wsD5. PathFinder misses these packages when PYTHONPATH is unset and sys.path[0] is scripts/e4_parity (direct script execution), so every regen-DAG subprocess imported wsD5 modules - SP6's candidate-validation fix and all post-wsD5 merged code NEVER EXECUTED inside the DAG. Reviewer/packet runs always set PYTHONPATH explicitly (PathFinder precedence), so packet evidence is unaffected.
attempt_5_root_cause.proof: direct run_lane invocation without PYTHONPATH crashed with traceback frames mixing integration run_lane.py with wsD5 lane_acceptance_artifacts.py (FileNotFoundError under wsD5); finder MAPPING captured verbatim before remediation.
attempt_5_root_cause.blast_radius: attempts 2-4 of this gate ran mixed wsD5 code and are invalidated as diagnostics of merged-code behavior (attempt_3 determinism result stands only as a property of the wsD5-code DAG). No accepted/promoted evidence affected: all promotion-grade packet evidence was produced under explicit PYTHONPATH and independently reviewed; the failing gate runs promoted nothing.
attempt_5_root_cause.remediation: pip install -e <integration checkout> --no-deps replaced the editable mapping (verified from neutral cwd: scripts/breadboard/agentic_coder_prototype resolve to breadboard_repo_integration_main_20260326). Stale wsD5 worktree left for closeout cleanup.

attempt_5.failing_stage: north_star_proof_packets, same 3 PIN_STALE (got 33e2aa4a), inner capture returncode 1 in ~5.2s

attempt_5_diagnosis_refinement.finding: Second independent cause: STALE SCRATCH REUSE. tmp/e4_regen_capture/breadboard_self_runtime_records_v1 retained kernel-event bytes (sha256:33e2aa4a...) captured by the wsD5-contaminated code path; run_lane capture stage treats existing scratch as reusable (~5s validate-only pass) instead of re-executing (~12-14s fresh capture). After rm -rf tmp/e4_regen_capture AND the venv repoint, capture reproduces the accepted bytes (sha256:4a4fe71c...) both with and without PYTHONPATH, all three north-star lanes ok.
attempt_5_diagnosis_refinement.verification: git blob == worktree == accepted (4a4fe71c) for bb.kernel_event.v2.jsonl at a131dc1b; no skip-worktree/attr filters; direct no-PYTHONPATH run post-cleanup: all lanes ok, scratch hash 4a4fe71c.
attempt_5_diagnosis_refinement.residual_defect_note: run_lane scratch reuse lacks staleness invalidation - stale scratch can mask true capture state across code changes. Out of campaign scope (bb-n4e estate class); flagged for backlog.

_seed_lane_support_estate: seed untracked accepted-capture estates wholesale (excl. target_home bulk)

_mirror_checkout_docs_tmp_to_workspace: hardlink checkout docs_tmp estate to candidate workspace root (adapter checkout-local + validator workspace-level conventions share one copy; symlinks fail post-resolve boundary checks in both directions)
catalog reads: codex_gpt55_capture_probe widened to directory seed
attempt_7.qualified_convergence: DAG hermeticity defects are resolved: every capture/generator stage and 9/21 reverify chains pass in an isolated candidate. Remaining exit-3/4 blockers are tracked-evidence data debt, not DAG defects: (a) F5 sidecar-stale ledger rows (feat_e7bfda921c4e30ed self_runtime 10 pins; claude/opencode north-star row hashes) - bb-fm6 scope per f5_seed_digest_resolution packet_disposition; (b) P3.1-P3.8 + P6.6 stale claim pins and scope semantics - bb-n4e (AM23b out-of-campaign residual). ct_scenarios/matrix/readiness and closure validators fail strictly downstream of the same pins. The canonical fixed-point gate cannot emit a generated commit until bb-fm6/bb-n4e land; per AM23b these are out of campaign scope.
